spec_match.rationale: The page now demonstrates the exact Chrome 148 contract: descendants match an ancestor by container-name alone, with no container-type, and the result is proven through computed style tokens.

interactive.rationale: Zone checkboxes and the remove-sidebar-name control update visible zones, container names, and a computed match grid for every component zone.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept copy and feature blurb describe identity/name-only @container matching without size containment, which is the behavior exercised on the page.

edge_cases.rationale: Removing the sidebar container name leaves that zone visible but changes its computed match token to base while the other named zones keep matching.

references.rationale: The concept has a labelled references section with ChromeStatus and CSS Conditional Rules Level 5 links.

design_system.rationale: The edited styles and visible snippets use shared CSS variables or computed values, button overrides set readable colors, and the page remains an interactive demo.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome Canary 151: sidebar/main/compact/featured zones reported name-only matches with container-type normal, removing the sidebar name changed its token to base, hiding featured updated display state, and console/network were clean.
